# Version 4.151.0 (Unreleased)
> **Performance and API improvements**

## Highlights

SkiaSharp 4.151.0 eliminates hot-path allocations in HarfBuzz and SKShaper, and adds span-friendly SKColor parsing overloads. Three allocation-free performance improvements land: HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse, SKFourByteTag.Parse, and SKShaper glyph results all drop per-call heap allocations. This line also adds ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads to SKColor parsing, removes the unnecessary unsafe modifier from GRContextOptions, and pulls in two rounds of upstream Skia m151 bug fixes.

## Breaking Changes

*None in this preview line.*

## API Surface

- **SKColor parsing is faster and span-friendly** — Parse and TryParse now use an allocation-free hex parser and add ReadOnlySpan<char> overloads, so callers can parse colors from larger buffers without slicing strings. ([#4345](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4345))
- **GRContextOptions no longer requires unsafe context** — The unnecessary unsafe modifier has been removed from GRContextOptions, allowing projects to configure GPU contexts without enabling unsafe code. ([#3991](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3991))

## Bug Fixes

- **Upstream Skia m151 bug fixes** — Pulls in two rounds of Skia m151 upstream fixes, including the SubRunAllocator use-after-free that caused GPU text rendering instability. ([#4312](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4312), [#4382](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4382))

## Lifecycle & Internals

- **Allocation-free parsing in HarfBuzz and Skia tag types** — HarfBuzzSharp.Tag.Parse and SKFourByteTag.Parse no longer allocate a char[4] on every call. SKShaper now reads glyph results through zero-copy spans, reducing GC pressure in text layout loops. ([#4408](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4408), [#4396](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4396), [#4388](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/4388))
